title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary: The reviewer found a stale test name in the documentation table at `docs/reference/keystone-crd.md:378` — the test was renamed to `TestIntegration_CELRejectsReplicasBelowMinimum` in the code but the docs still reference the old name `TestIntegration_CELRejectsReplicasZero`. Update the table row to use the new name and corrected description.

code_comments.body:
**Stale test name in documentation table**

The table entry references `TestIntegration_CELRejectsReplicasZero`, but this test was renamed to `TestIntegration_CELRejectsReplicasBelowMinimum` in `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/integration_test.go` as part of this very PR (to address the previous review comment). The documentation wasn't updated to match.

```suggestion
| `TestIntegration_CELRejectsReplicasBelowMinimum` | Minimum constraint | `replicas = -1` (note: 0 is converted to 3 by the defaulting webhook, so -1 is used) | Invalid/Forbidden |
```
